name: Architext 1.7.0 Rust Rewrite (Zero Node)
updateSource: 2026-06-15 zero-Node mandate: viewer rewrite (Rust→WASM UI) pulled into 1.7.0; build-time Node residual revoked; npm demoted to opt-in bridge.
summary: Reimplement the entire product in Rust with NO Node dependency surviving — runtime AND build. One Rust routing core (native + WASM); validation, serve layer, and lifecycle ported; AND the React/Vite viewer rewritten as a Rust → WASM UI (Leptos/Dioxus/Yew, no JSX/Vite/npm) reusing the engine directly and implementing the Stitch design language. Native installers are the Node-free default; npm is an opt-in compatibility bridge only. Minor release: JSON data contract and CLI surface preserved exactly, proven by a byte-parity migration gate and a validation conformance corpus. Migration tooling (parity harness, ajv conformance, JS tests) is transitional and deleted with the JS code.

required.title: Rewrite the viewer as a Rust → WASM UI (no Node build)
required.summary: Replace React/Vite with a Rust web framework (Leptos/Dioxus/Yew) compiled to WASM: all nine modes (Flows, Sequence, C4, Deployment, Data/Risks, Repo Tree, Blast Radius, Release Truth, Rules), the diagram canvas (fluid, pan/zoom), editors, and inspectors. Reuses architext-routing directly (no JSON/WASM string boundary). No JSX, Vite, or npm anywhere in the build. The single largest piece of the zero-Node mandate; runs in parallel with the engine port.

required.title: Port the routing/planning engine to a Rust crate (native + WASM)
required.summary: Translate viewer/src/routing/* into the architext-routing crate (cdylib + rlib), compiled native for serve-side precompute and to WASM for the browser worker. Internally cohesive port done as one unit; uses IndexMap/IndexSet where JS insertion order drives tiebreaks. Realizes the engine-in-scope decision that distinguishes this from the original Go-only plan.

required.title: js_compat determinism primitives (V8 parity)
required.summary: Reproduce the exact JS semantics the router depends on: js_number_to_string (V8 Number::toString via ryu-js), js_round (half toward +inf), UTF-16 string sort + locale compare, and libm-backed hypot on every target so native and WASM floats are identical. The narrow surface where byte-parity lives.

required.title: Differential fingerprint harness (JS vs Rust-WASM)
required.summary: Extend route-parity-fingerprint to run both engines over every corpus flow x view and diff sha256 fingerprints (points + d + label positions), driving the port flow-by-flow RED to GREEN. The one-time migration gate proving the Rust engine matches the outgoing JS engine byte-for-byte.

required.title: Port validation with a conformance corpus
required.summary: Port the ajv 2020-12 schema validation + custom reference checks to Rust (jsonschema crate + ported logic) in architext-core. A conformance corpus of accept/reject fixtures proves verdict parity between the outgoing ajv validator and the Rust validator, error-for-error.

required.title: Port the serve HTTP layer
required.summary: Reproduce the 11 /api endpoints, the mutation-token + write-lock model on the 6 mutating POSTs, the SSE /api/data-events channel, file watching, the plan precompute farm (sha256(plan_input_key), generation-based invalidation, 200-miss contract), and the embedded viewer/dist.

required.title: Port the CLI + lifecycle commands
required.summary: Port validate, serve, sync, doctor, prompt, status, and migrate to a clap-based Rust binary, preserving the CLI surface exactly. Includes the lifecycle transforms (schema migrations, doctor repairs, instruction-rule migration, target layout).

required.title: Parallelize the plan precompute farm
required.summary: Fan the precompute farm across a worker pool (plans are independent), warming default view x flow pairs first; the native equivalent of the deferred parallel-plan-precompute roadmap item, mapping onto a rayon pool (and goroutine-style fan-out the Rust core makes cheap).

required.title: Signed native binaries + CI build matrix
required.summary: Cross-compile and sign per-platform binaries (macOS arm64/x64, Linux x64/arm64, Windows x64); ship via Homebrew, a curl installer, and GitHub releases as the recommended path for new users. CI builds the WASM artifact + viewer/dist, embeds, and runs the parity + conformance gates before publish.

required.title: npm thin-launcher upgrade bridge
required.summary: Keep publishing @robotaccomplice/architext as a thin launcher whose per-platform Rust binaries ship as optionalDependencies (esbuild/Biome model), so a plain npm update brings every prior npm user onto the Rust build with no manual step. The npm artifact carries no runtime JS beyond binary resolution/spawn.

required.title: UI design-language refactor (chrome-only, audited)
required.summary: Apply the new design language (locked in viewer/DESIGN.md, explored via Stitch) to the viewer as a chrome-only refactor that does not change diagram-layout-input defaults. UX-LED: the full user experience drives decisions — beauty is subordinate to usability, workflow integrity, and functional parity. COMPLETION GATE (rule design-language-fidelity-audit): not 'done' until a post-implementation audit — in the live-rendered UI, using the design-system skills — verifies (1) UX: every task still works, is discoverable, and is no slower than before, and (2) faithfulness to viewer/DESIGN.md + coherency across ALL modes (shared tokens, typography, the nav/canvas/inspector triad, fluid canvas, ARCHITEXT wordmark, state-not-role-hue). Partial/per-screen application, or a prettier UI that degrades a workflow, does not count.

required.title: Diagram spacing controls
required.summary: In-viewer controls for column width and sequence-diagram row spacing, surfacing laneWidth/rowGap (and node size) overrides the planner already accepts as input. Folded out of the 1.6.x stream into this UI refactor; does not move the parity baseline (defaults unchanged), so it is parity-safe.

workstreams.name: Routing engine to Rust (native + WASM)
workstreams.summary: Port the routing/planning engine to one Rust crate compiled native + WASM, proven byte-identical to the outgoing JS engine via the differential fingerprint harness. The highest-risk, highest-value workstream; everything else is gated behind it.

workstreams.name: Validation parity
workstreams.summary: Port schema + reference validation to Rust with a conformance corpus proving verdict parity with ajv.

workstreams.name: Native serve + CLI
workstreams.summary: Port the serve HTTP layer and CLI/lifecycle commands to Rust, embed the viewer, and parallelize the plan farm.

workstreams.name: Distribution & npm bridge
workstreams.summary: Signed native installers as the primary path; npm thin-launcher bridge so existing installs auto-upgrade onto the Rust build.

workstreams.name: Viewer rewrite (Rust → WASM UI)
workstreams.summary: Replace the React/Vite viewer with a Rust → WASM UI (Leptos/Dioxus/Yew) — no JSX, Vite, or npm — reusing architext-routing directly and implementing the locked Stitch design language. The largest workstream; required by the zero-Node mandate.

milestones.label: Rust engine byte-identical to JS across the corpus
Native serve + CLI with no Node runtime
Facelift passes the live fidelity + coherency audit (all modes)
